name: Bynder Digital Asset Management API
what_it_does: Bynder digital asset management REST API for marketing, brand, and creative teams to automate asset storage, metadata management, rights management, distribution, and creative workflow within a centralized brand asset portal. Enables AI agents to manage asset upload and metadata tagging for content repository automation, handle asset search and retrieval by metadata filters for content delivery automation, access brand template and creative content generation for brand consistency automation, retrieve asset version management and approval workflow for creative ops automation, manage digital rights and asset expiry for rights management automation, handle CDN-optimized asset delivery for content distribution automation, access brand guidelines and asset usage analytics for brand governance automation, retrieve collection and album management for campaign asset organization automation, manage integration with creative tools (Adobe Creative Cloud, Figma) for creative workflow automation, and integrate Bynder with CMS, PIM, ecommerce, and marketing automation platforms for end-to-end brand asset lifecycle management.
use_cases: Retrieving brand assets from content delivery automation agents
Tagging assets from metadata management agents
Managing asset rights from rights tracking agents
Distributing campaign assets from marketing automation agents
not_for: Small business basic file storage (Bynder targets enterprise/mid-market)
Code and software version control (use GitHub)
Document workflow management (use Box or SharePoint)
best_when: An enterprise or mid-market marketing team wanting AI agents to automate brand asset retrieval, metadata management, rights tracking, creative workflow approvals, and multi-channel content distribution within Bynder's digital asset management platform.
avoid_when: ASSET COPYRIGHT RIGHTS CLEARANCE BEFORE DISTRIBUTION: Bynder tracks rights metadata for assets but rights enforcement is configuration-dependent; automated asset distribution must verify rights status and expiry before distribution; automated distribution of rights-expired assets creates copyright infringement liability; implement rights expiry check in automated asset distribution workflows. GDPR IMAGES CONTAINING IDENTIFIABLE PERSONS: Brand assets containing identifiable individuals (models, employees in photos) may require model releases under GDPR for commercial use; automated asset tagging and distribution must flag assets with identifiable persons for release verification; automated distribution of personal image assets without verified model release creates GDPR data subject rights exposure. LARGE ASSET BINARY UPLOAD BANDWIDTH CONSIDERATIONS: Bynder asset upload for large creative files (RAW images, video, 3D assets) may have significant upload bandwidth and time requirements; automated bulk asset ingestion must account for upload latency for large binary files; automated asset migration workflows should implement chunked upload and progress tracking for large file sets.

auth.notes: Bynder uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. REST API with JSON. Amsterdam, Netherlands HQ with US offices. Founded 2013 by Chris Hall, Bob van Luijt, and Roel Heijnen. Backed by Insight Partners ($52M+ raised). 4,000+ customers in 50+ countries. Products: Digital asset management, brand portal, creative workflow, content templates, CDN. Integration: Adobe Creative Cloud, Figma, Salesforce, HubSpot, WordPress, Sitecore, Shopify. GDPR. SOC2. ISO27001. Competes with Widen (Acquia), Canto, and Brandfolder (Smartsheet) for enterprise DAM.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise annual subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user; storage and bandwidth tiers; custom enterprise pricing; implementation services
pricing.notes: Amsterdam Netherlands. Insight Partners backed. Per-user annual subscription. Enterprise pricing. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; asset and metadata queries throttled; webhooks for asset upload, approval, and rights events

known_agent_gotchas: ASSET PORTAL DOMAIN REQUIRED FOR API CALLS: Bynder API calls require the customer's specific portal domain (e.g., company.bynder.com) not a generic endpoint; automated API integrations must use the customer's assigned Bynder portal domain; API calls to generic Bynder domain without portal subdomain will fail with authentication error

METADATA PROPERTY CONFIGURATION IS INSTANCE-SPECIFIC: Bynder metadata fields are configured per customer portal; automated metadata tagging must use property IDs from the specific portal configuration; generic metadata field names may differ across Bynder portals; retrieve portal metadata schema before automated tagging deployment

ASSET DOWNLOAD DERIVATIVE SELECTION FOR FORMAT OPTIMIZATION: Bynder generates multiple derivatives (thumbnails, web, original) for each asset; automated asset delivery should request appropriate derivative for use case; automated download of original high-resolution assets for web use creates bandwidth waste; specify derivative parameter in automated download requests

RIGHTS MANAGEMENT EXPIRY DATE vs GEOGRAPHIC RESTRICTION: Bynder rights management supports both expiry date and geographic usage restriction; automated asset distribution workflows must check both expiry date AND geographic rights for multi-market campaigns; automated distribution checking only expiry date misses geographic rights restriction for regional campaign compliance

WEBHOOK DELIVERY RETRY AND IDEMPOTENCY: Bynder webhooks may deliver duplicate events during retry; automated webhook consumers must implement idempotency using asset ID and event type; automated processing of duplicate webhook events creates duplicate workflow actions without idempotency check
agent_readiness.security_notes: Digital asset management. GDPR, SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. EU/US. Brand assets, rights, and creative content data.

security_score_components.security_notes: DAM platform. GDPR, SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. EU/US. Brand asset, rights, and creative content data.
